task_completion.description: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main interface of the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Ibrutinib is selected as the cancer drug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Ibrutinib' is visible as a selected item in the cancer drugs list or search interface within the app.

task_completion.description: Ketoconazole is selected as a co-medication.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Ketoconazole' is visible as a selected item in the co-medications list within the app. (Other co-medications like fluconazole or acenocoumarol may also be selected).

task_completion.description: The interaction results summary screen is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app shows a summary list of drug interactions with traffic-light severity ratings (e.g., Red for Ketoconazole).

task_completion.description: The Interaction Details screen for Ibrutinib and Ketoconazole is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the detailed interaction report specifically between Ibrutinib and Ketoconazole, indicating a severe rating (e.g., 'Do Not Coadminister' / Red).

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the UI of the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app, not a web browser or a text document.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app by searching for the interaction on the web.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no web browser open with search queries like 'Ibrutinib and Ketoconazole interaction'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent navigated through the app's menus and screens rather than using terminal commands to extract app data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Ibrutinib is the cancer drug. The three co-medications to screen are acenocoumarol, fluconazole, and ketoconazole. Ketoconazole is a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor and has the most severe drug-drug interaction rating with ibrutinib ('Do Not Coadminister' / Red). Fluconazole is moderate, and acenocoumarol has no significant pharmacokinetic interaction. The final goal is to reach the Interaction Details screen for Ibrutinib and Ketoconazole within the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.
